etymology.description: 純(純) (chún, "pure") + 素(素) (sù, "plain; vegetarian") + 食(食) (shí, "food") + 者(者) (zhě, "person") indicate a person who eats pure and plain food, i.e., one who follows a vegan diet.
etymology.interpretation: 純(純) (chún, "pure") implies the exclusion of animal products, aligning with a purely plant-based diet. 素(素) (sù, "plain; vegetarian") refers to foods derived from plants without animal involvement. 食(食) (shí, "food") reinforces the dietary aspect, while 者(者) (zhě, "person") indicates an individual who practices this lifestyle. The combination distinctly describes someone who abstains from all animal products, a vegan.

examples.chinese: 我的朋友是一个严格的纯素食者(我的朋友是一個嚴格的純素食者)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de péngyǒu shì yīgè yángé de chúnsùshízhě.
examples.english: My friend is a strict vegan.

examples.chinese: 作为纯素食者(作為純素食者)，她总是寻找新的食谱(她總是尋找新的食譜)。
examples.pinyin: Zuòwéi chúnsùshízhě, tā zǒng shì xúnzhǎo xīn de shípǔ.
examples.english: As a vegan, she is always looking for new recipes.

examples.chinese: 他开了一家纯素食者餐厅(他開了一家純素食者餐廳)。
examples.pinyin: Tā kāi le yī jiā chúnsùshízhě cāntīng.
